role: Operator-facing React component that renders a build-intent's persisted blast radius as an interactive concentric-ring ripple and lets the operator tick and approve which related code addresses widen the editable scope.
capabilities: Render persisted blast_radius rings (structural_dependents, structural_dependencies, co_reference, semantic_neighbours) as an SVG ripple
Display every blast-radius member as a tick-able chip in a per-ring legend
Toggle ticked state via legend chip or on-ring node to widen the build's editable scope
Call api.approveReach to persist the operator-approved reach expansion
Optimistically mark approved members as 'in reach' after a successful API call
Show an explicit empty state when the radius contains no related code
Disable the approve action until at least one member is ticked (default-narrow behavior)
Switch between three aesthetic variants A/B/C with shared behavior but different SVG geometry/layout
Apply a visual-only per-ring density cap and '+N' overflow chip (variant B)
Render inline ring labels for variant B
Guard navigation so only ui:// members trigger onNavigate; code:// members stay inert
Truncate long member addresses to a legible leaf label with full address in title tooltip
substance_why: It implements a complete UI surface: SVG ripple rendering, tick/approve state machine, API call, optimistic updates, empty state, error handling, and three aesthetic variants.
interactions_present: Tick/un-tick a blast-radius member via a legend chip button or an on-ring SVG node
Approve reach by clicking the disabled-until-ticked approve button, which calls api.approveReach
Switch the A/B/C aesthetic variant via a segmented control button group
Navigate to a ui:// member address by clicking its chip label (onNavigate callback)
View native title tooltips on rings, nodes, chips, overflow marks, and controls
interactions_missing: Navigation for non-ui:// (code://) members is explicitly reserved and inert
No search, filter, select-all, or bulk-clear affordances in the legend
No confirmation step before committing the reach expansion
No retry or cancel affordance after an approval error
Variant selector choice is local-only and not persisted
On-ring SVG nodes are mouse-clickable but not keyboard-accessible
No loading or skeleton state while blast_radius data resolves
No explicit handling of malformed d.id or missing payload beyond optional access
performance_risks: Legend always renders every member as a DOM chip regardless of the SVG density cap, so large blast radii create many interactive nodes
SVG ring nodes and legend chips rebuild arrays and closures on every render without memoization
useMemo depends on the br object identity, so unstable identity causes recomputation each render
approvedAll Set is reconstructed every render and iterated inside nested loops
Leaf truncation and navigable checks run inline for every chip/node without memoization
reuse_why: The tick/approve state machine, default-narrow policy, and API integration are concrete and worth keeping, but the visual surface, variant geometry, and legend rendering should be rebuilt and split for the redesign.
decomposition_hints: Split the approval state machine and api.approveReach call into a dedicated container/hook (e.g., useReachApproval)
Extract the SVG ripple renderer into its own component driven by a geometry config object
Extract the legend chip list into a separate component with room for search/filter and bulk actions
Extract the A/B/C variant selector into a small presentational control
Separate code:// vs ui:// navigation resolution into a utility or hook once the reserved design call is resolved
Move VARIANTS geometry constants and RINGS metadata into dedicated config modules
